2606.11916.title: Characterizing Software Aging in GPU-Based LLM Serving Systems
2606.11916.paper_summary: This paper proposes an empirical methodology to study software aging in GPU-based LLM serving systems. Traditional aging studies focus on CPU-centric software with relatively regular workloads; LLM serving is different, spanning a Python host and a CUDA device, handling requests whose cost varies by orders of magnitude, and relying on rapidly evolving software stacks. We run a 216-hour campaign across six co-located deployments under identical stress conditions, monitor host, device, and client metrics in parallel, and apply a statistical pipeline that accounts for autocorrelation and multiple testing. Our results reveal statistically significant memory aging in all deployments, with leak rates strongly dependent on the serving runtime and deployment configuration. Beyond these findings, we provide a reproducible framework that opens a research direction at the intersection of the software aging and rejuvenation and LLM serving communities.
2606.11916.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文提出了一種實證方法來研究基於 GPU 的 LLM 服務系統中的軟體老化。傳統的老化研究專注於以 CPU 為中心的軟體，並且工作負載相對規律；而 LLM 服務則不同，跨越 Python 主機和 CUDA 設備，處理請求的成本變化幅度極大，並依賴於快速演變的軟體堆疊。我們在六個共同部署的環境中，在相同的壓力條件下進行了216小時的實驗，並平行監控主機、設備和客戶端的指標，應用考慮自相關和多重測試的統計管道。我們的結果顯示所有部署中均存在統計上顯著的記憶體老化，洩漏率強烈依賴於服務運行時和部署配置。除了這些發現，我們提供了一個可重現的框架，開啟了在軟體老化與復甦以及 LLM 服務社群交集的研究方向。
2606.11916.author: Domenico Cotroneo et.al.
